title: Urban Traffic Flow

analysis.object: T242: Urban traffic flow depth analysis

traffic_wave.description: Stop-and-go wave: backward propagation at ~20 km/h
traffic_wave.reason: Jamiton/traffic wave = EML-3 oscillation propagating backward

lws_model.reason: Conservation law with algebraic q(ρ) = EML-2

level_of_service.description: LOS A-F classification based on density/capacity ratio

signal_optimization.description: Green wave timing: coordinated signal phases

analysis.traffic_wave_prediction: Does the framework predict when traffic waves form? YES. Traffic waves form when density crosses the EML-2→EML-3 boundary. Below critical density: LWS (EML-2) — stable flow, predictable. Above critical density: Jamiton waves (EML-3) — oscillatory, partially predictable. Gridlock: EML-3 × high density = EML-∞ phase transition. Rush hour = predictable EML-3 phenomenon (periodic daily cycle). Flash gridlock = EML-∞ (incident-triggered phase transition).

analysis.verdict: Traffic waves: EML-3. LWS model: EML-2. Gridlock: EML-∞.
analysis.theorem: T242: Traffic Flow Depth — waves EML-3, gridlock EML-∞ phase transition

key_theorem: T242: Traffic Flow Depth (S521). LWS model: EML-2 (conservation). Traffic waves: EML-3 (Jamiton oscillation). Rush hour: predictable EML-3 (periodic daily cycle). Flash gridlock: EML-∞ (incident-triggered phase transition). EML predicts: EML-2→EML-3 density threshold = wave formation onset.
rabbit_hole_log: T242: Rush hour = predictable EML-3; flash gridlock = EML-∞
